Data.Description: Manage UiPath Orchestrator folders. Folders organize and scope resources (processes, jobs, assets, queues) with role-based access. Most commands require --folder-path or --folder-key to target a specific folder.

Subcommands.Name: list [options]
Subcommands.Description: List folders the current user has access to. Use --all to list all folders in the tenant (supports filtering and sorting).

Subcommands.Name: create [options] <name>
Subcommands.Description: Create a new folder. Folders scope processes, jobs, and resources with separate permissions. Use --parent to nest inside an existing folder.

Subcommands.Name: get [options] <folder-key-or-path>
Subcommands.Description: Get folder details by key (GUID) or path. Returns display name, key (GUID), path, type, and permissions. Use 'folders list' to find folder keys.

Subcommands.Name: delete [options] <folder-key-or-path>
Subcommands.Description: Delete a folder by key (GUID) or path. Fails if the folder contains entities or user associations. Move or remove contents before deleting.

Subcommands.Name: move [options] <folder-key-or-path>
Subcommands.Description: Move a folder to a new parent in the folder hierarchy. Provide the target parent folder path or key. Use --root to move to the top level.

Subcommands.Name: edit [options] <folder-key-or-path>
Subcommands.Description: Edit folder properties (display name, description). Use 'folders get' to see current values before editing.

Subcommands.Name: runtimes [options] <folder-key-or-path>
Subcommands.Description: List runtime allocations for a folder. Shows how many runtime slots of each type (Unattended, Attended, NonProduction, etc.) are total, connected, and available in the specified folder. Useful for checking capacity before starting jobs.

GlobalOptions.Description: Write logs to file instead of stderr
